main_turf.bias_notes: Clockwise. A massive, wide, and sprawling galloping track widely regarded as one of the fairest and most reliable turf surfaces in Queensland. Features broad, sweeping turns and a generous 400m home straight. The track's wide layout means every horse gets an unhindered opportunity to find clear running, virtually eliminating extreme pace biases.
main_turf.post_bias: Extremely fair across all distances. Long run-ins from major starting chutes (like the 1200m and 1400m starts) give wide-drawn horses ample time to establish position and find cover without burning excessive fuel.

synthetic_polytrack.bias_notes: Clockwise. Positioned inside the main turf oval, this high-performance all-weather synthetic track features tighter corners and a slightly shorter straight. It provides uniform footing but creates a more grinding environment that favors tactical early speed and rail-skimming runs.
synthetic_polytrack.post_bias: Inside positions hold a moderate advantage due to the tighter turning configuration of the inner synthetic ring. Wide draws require tactical gate speed to cross over effectively.

handicapping_angles.the_spacious_gallop: On the turf, aggressively downgrade one-dimensional front-runners who rely on tight turns to pinch races. The long 400m straight and wide lanes heavily reward large-striding gallopers and genuine 'Swoopers' with high baseline stamina.
handicapping_angles.metro_alternate_meetings: Sunshine Coast frequently hosts high-quality Friday night cards and serves as a major provincial alternate for Brisbane metropolitan stables. Form from meetings here measures up strongly against midweek metropolitan form lines.
handicapping_angles.synthetic_track_inversion: When turf meetings are transferred to the Polytrack due to severe weather, shift completely away from standard grass turn-of-foot metrics. Prioritize synthetic specialists, high tactical gate speed, and peak fitness profiles.
